title: Amazon | SDE-1 | Bangalore
content:
**Education:** MCA (Top NIT), 2024 Batch  
**Experience:** 1 year 7 months  
**Current Role:** SDE, Product Based  

Company: Amazon  
Role: SDE-1 (L4)  
Location: Bangalore  
Offer Date: April 2026  

--- Compensation Breakdown ---

Base Salary: ₹19,17,000  

Joining Bonus:
- Year 1: ₹6,47,000  
- Year 2: ₹5,18,000  

RSUs: ₹15,56,000  
(Vesting: 5% / 15% / 40% / 40% over 4 years)

---

Approx Total Compensation:

- Year 1: ~₹26.4 LPA (Base + Joining Bonus + RSUs)  
- Year 2: ~₹26.7 LPA (Base + Joining Bonus + RSUs)  
- Year 3: ~₹25.4 LPA (Base + RSUs)  
- Year 4: ~₹25.4 LPA (Base + RSUs)  

---

Notes:
- RSU values are approximate and depend on stock price at vesting  
- Performance bonus (if any) is not included in the above calculations  

---

If you want to know more about the interview process, let me know in the comments — I’ll share a detailed post.

Edit:

Interview Experience: [Click Here](https://leetcode.com/discuss/post/8029194/amazon-interview-sde-1-selected-by-anony-qatd/)

title: JioHotstar vs. Airbase (Paylocity) vs. Keka | SDE-2 | Help me decide!
content:
**Hi everyone**,

I’m currently an SDE-2 at a Fintech startup with ~2.5 years of experience. I primarily work on Backend systems (Java, Spring Boot, Distributed Systems). I have managed to secure three offers and need your help evaluating them based on Work-Life Balance (WLB), long-term growth, and total compensation.

**Offer 1: Airbase (by Paylocity)**

Role: SDE-2

Location: 100% Remote (Forever)

Fixed CTC: ₹25.17 LPA

Variable/Stocks: None (All Fixed)

Pros: Pure product firm, high fixed monthly in-hand, global engineering standards, zero commute.

**Offer 2: JioHotstar (Viewer Experience Team)**

Role: SDE-2

Location: Hybrid (Mumbai/Bangalore)

Fixed CTC: ₹25.20 LPA

Retention Bonus: ₹3 Lakhs (₹1.5L in May 2027 + ₹1.5L in May 2028)

Stocks: ₹3 Lakhs per year

Total Year 1 Comp: ~₹28.2 LPA (Fixed + Stocks)

Pros: Massive scale (millions of concurrent users), brand name, high-visibility projects.

Cons: Commute, reputation for high pressure during live events, bonus is locked for 2+ years.

**Offer 3: Keka HR**

Role: SDE-2

Location: Hybrid (2 days office)

Fixed CTC: ₹21 LPA

Pros: Very strong culture, stable domain.

Cons: Lowest CTC of the three.

**My Dilemma:**
I value the 100% Remote flexibility of Airbase, especially since the fixed pay is almost identical to JioHotstar's fixed. However, the JioHotstar scale is tempting for a Backend Engineer.

How is the current culture at JioHotstar after the merger? Is the "toxic culture" feedback real for the Viewer Experience team?

Does the Airbase brand carry weight for future switches to Tier-1 product companies?

Which one would you choose?

Please tag people if you know from these companies!!

#engineering #corporate #JobOffer #SDE2 #CareerAdvice #SalaryNegotiation #SoftwareEngineer #LeetCode #InterviewExperience #Compensation

title: Walmart SSE Offer | Repost | Need Advice.
content:
Hi community, I recently received offer from Walmart for SSE, IN4.
YOE: 5.9 years.

Current CTC:
Base: 25L
RSU: 25L per year
overall 50L per year.

Offered:
Base : 32L
Bonus: 6.4L
RSU: 9.5L with 1/3rd vesting in 1st year.

Overall CTC comes to be around 41L per year which is lesser than my current CTC.
HR is not budging for more saying its is 30% hike on base and not willing to give more.

I know this is serious lowball but want to understand from community what should be done here? 
- Is this the range for SSE in walmart? 
- How do I negotiate with HR?
- Do Walmart provide one time joining bonus/new Hire stock to offset the loss?

title: Kotak Mahindra Bank | SDE 2 | Offer Delay
content:
I recently went through the interview process with Kotak Mahindra Bank via a staffing agency recruiter and wanted to understand timelines + compensation expectations.

#### 🧾 Interview Timeline:
- R1 (Bar Raiser): 3rd Week Feb → Positive feedback (after ~3 days)
- R2: 4th Week Feb → Positive feedback (after ~1 week)
- R3: Scheduled 3rd Week March → Rescheduled (interviewer unavailable)
- R3 (Rescheduled): 4th Week March
- Final Feedback: Positive (2nd April 2026)

#### Current Situation:
- External recruiter confirmed positive feedback across all rounds and verbally asked for current compensation, competing offers etc.
- Mentioned next step: HR discussion (compensation + joining)
- However it’s been a while, and HR round hasn’t been scheduled yet. I’m regularly following up with recruiter and he ask me to not worry, that he will schedule once he gets available slots.

#### My Background:
- ~4 YOE
- My last working day in my previous company was in the last week of March (already communicated during process).
- I had another offer from a reputed product based company with: ~₹45.5 LPA fixed base ~₹4L joining bonus. (Before that it was ~35 CTC)
- I have recently joined that company, but I am still interested in Kotak primarily due to location flexibility.

#### Questions:
1. How long does it usually take from final positive feedback to HR discussion / offer rollout at Kotak? Can anyone share their rough timelines who went through the same?
2. What compensation can I realistically expect from Kotak given:
Their policy: Max(30% hike, competing offer).
3. How are increments, bonuses, and promotion cycles at Kotak?
4. What are the key employee benefits/perks at Kotak (insurance, bonus, WLB, etc.)?
5. How is Kotak for long-term career growth (2–3 years horizon)?
especially from: Learning perspective and Compensation growth.
6. Is early switching (within weeks of joining) viewed negatively in background checks or future hiring?


[Posted on behalf of friend]

title: JPMC SDE 2 - Compensation
content:
Hi everyone, 

Offer received on Feb 2026

College : Tier 3 (2023 grad)

Experience : 2 years 9 months
Previous Company: Support role at FAANG
Past CTC: 25 LPA (Base: 14 LPA)

 -----


JPMorgan Chase & Co SDE 2 CTC:

Base : 29 LPA
Performance bonus : 0 - 12% of base ~ 3 L
Joining bonus: 7 lakhs (It was basically the value of my unvested stock compensation at my previous company. Since JPMC doesn’t offer stock-based compensation, they provided an equivalent amount in cash instead)

Total CTC (for 1st year): 29 + 3 + 7 = 39 LPA



Rejected the offer

Just want to get your thoughts on whether the offer was lowballed or actually a good one.

title: Kotak Mahindra bank-SDE2
content:
I gave 4 interview rounds for a Kotak SDE2 role within an interval of 1–2 weeks.

Right after the 4th round, HR told me that the feedback was positive, but detailed feedback from the interviewer was still pending.

After around 12–15 days, the interviewer finally submitted the feedback. Post that, HR informed me that my profile had been forwarded to the HR head for further processing.

However, it’s now been more than 10–12 days since then, and there has been no communication.

I just wanted to know how much time it usually takes to get an offer in such cases. The offer hasn’t been discussed yet, but I’m honestly waiting for it.

Please let me know if anyone else facing the same

I’m looking for a dedicated practice partner to prepare together for DSA + System Design.

Solving LeetCode problems together
Regular mock interviews (DSA + LLD/HLD System Design)
Sharing strategies, discussing patterns & helping each other improve

Targetting companies at least 17 - 25 LPA base salary range
Strongly Dedicated for next 3 months Prep

About me

working on early stage startup
trying to be consistent with DSA & System Design.

Fixed : 43
1st Year bonus: 19 LPA
2nd year bonus: 14.5 LPA
Stocks : 232 RSU vested over 4 years ( 5%, 15%, 40%, 40%)
Annual CTC roughly ~65LPA

Amazon's WLB and culture could be shit, but nothing can be shittier compared to what I had in ServiceNow. Atleast my team is working on real scale and engineering problems rather than slopping shit glidescript.

#servicenow #amazon

title: Noting Really happens when you Report someone on the leaderboard
content:
# Leetcode Please do Something About THIS 

---


i have reported multiple cheaters that copy code from **LLMs** during the contest that can easily be seen in their contest recordings ,
 fun fact is that sometimes they dont even remove comments , but nothing really happens after that , the cheaters keep doing the same time and LC doesnt ban them even after they are reported.

title: 2 YOE – Product FTE (14.5 LPA) vs Amazon Contract (18 LPA) vs Current 5.5 LPA – Need advice
content:

I’ve received two offers and I’m a bit confused about what to choose:
Offer 1 (Product company – Full-time)

14.5 LPA fixed + ~1.16 L bonus

Stable, full-time position

Offer 2 (Amazon – Contract)
18 LPA
Contract role (no guarantee of FTE conversion)
Better brand value

title: JPMC SDE 2 vs Walmart SWE 3
content:
Hi,

I’m looking for advice on choosing between two offers. My long-term goal is to transition into a FAANG company. 

YOE: 3 years
Current CTC: 18.5 LPA (Fixed: 16.5L)
Location: Banglore

Offer 1: JPMC

* Role: Software Engineer II
* Total Compensation: ₹30 LPA
* Fixed: ₹27 LPA (including PF)
* Variable: 10–12%
* Location: Hyderabad
* Additional: Relocation assistance provided


Offer 2: Walmart

* Role: SWE III
* Total Compensation: ₹40 LPA
* Base: ₹26.2 LPA (includes ₹1.2L PF)
* Bonus: ~20% (~₹5 LPA, performance-based)
* Stocks: ~₹9L (vesting over 3 years)
* First-Year Compensation: ~₹35 LPA
* Location: Bangalore


I’m inclined towards JPMC primarily because I want to relocate to Hyderabad. I haven’t negotiated with JPMC yet, what would be a reasonable compensation target to ask for?

Also, what is the average annual hike at JPMC?

Will share the interview experience in the next post

title: Job Switch - CISCO || SAP
content:
Hi Community,
I am currently working at SAP Labs, Its been 1.9 years.

Offer in hand:

Company: Cisco
- Role: SDE2 (G6)
- Base: 21 LPA
- Stocks: $10K
- Bonus: None

Current compensation:

- Company: SAP Labs
- Role: SDE1 (Associate Developer)
- Base: 12–13 LPA
- Stocks: ~5 Lakh
- Bonus: 6 Lakh (paid over 2 years)

One important factor: if I leave SAP now, I’ll need to repay the full 6 Lakh bonus (plus tax implications).

I’m trying to decide:

- Is it worth switching to Cisco now considering compensation, role jump, and brand value?
- Or does it make more sense to stay, complete 2 years, and avoid the repayment—at the cost of missing this opportunity?

Thanks,
Looking forward for your suggestion

title: 🚨 Need Career Advice | Service-Based vs Startup 🚨
content:
I’m currently working in a service-based company, but the salary is quite low. Recently, I got an offer from a startup offering almost a 100% hike on my current salary.

Now I’m stuck in a dilemma:

👉 Stay in my current company for **stability & job security**
👉 Or move to a startup for **higher pay & faster growth (but risk involved)**

I know startups can offer great learning opportunities, ownership, and exposure—but they also come with uncertainty. On the other hand, service-based companies feel safer but growth (both salary & learning) can be slow.

For those who’ve been in a similar situation:

- What did you choose and why?
- Looking back, would you do anything differently?

Would really appreciate honest advice.

title: Juspay | fresher | backend software developer selection process
content:
# Compensation
**base**: 14 to 16 lakhs
**relocation** or joining (depending on which city you are in): 1 lakh
**retention**: 8 lakhs, 2 after completion of every year

# Interview process overview

It is a long process, not a quick 3 step interview. They value logical thinking, and depth seeking mindset with frequent tweaks in problems to check your thought process. 

## Round 1: MCQ

MCQ round of 30-45 mins (around 20 questions) on computer science, physics, chemistry, maths and logical reasoning

Higher number of questions from CS, logical reasoning background.

## Round 2: DSA

Standard DSA round with 3 questions in around 75-90 mins. Medium difficulty.

## Round 3: Hackathon part A

You will be given one question and 3 hours to solve it, the question will be based on graphs and trees.

It is a machine coding round, not an interview

## Round 4: Elimination round

This will be an interview where you are asked one/two dsa questions. Focus on BFS/DFS, recursion, and graphs/trees. 

## Round 5: Hackathon part B

This will be a full day (6-7 hours) round where you are assigned a person from the company. The first half is spent discussing your part A solution, asking questions about your approach and optimising from there

The second part is spent on operating systems fundamentals and logical thinking. It is more important to think in a structured manner rather than show memorised knowledge

Questions will be tweaked so that standard solutions dont work, need to have clear critical thinking

## Round 6: Final round

Once you clear this, you will be called for an inperson interview where your genral CS knowledge is checked. Computer networks, your thinking process and maybe one DSA question. 

## Product manager round (optional)

Basically a tem matching round. This is not done for everyone. The purpose to understand your interests, what you would like to work and your working style and find a team that suits this.
